title: Authors and Citation
text: Baldrighi G, Tuomaala J (2025). MSNeuroLab: Statistical analyses procedures lab. R package version 0.0.0.9000.
code: @Manual{,   title = {MSNeuroLab: Statistical analyses procedures from our lab},   author = {Giulia Nicole Baldrighi and Joel Tuomaala},   year = {2025},   note = {R package version 0.0.0.9000}, }

what: Statistical analyses procedures from our lab
title: Statistical analyses procedures from our lab
text: Repository statistical analyses

title: Statistical analyses procedures from our lab
text: MSNeuroLab provides comprehensive set tools statistical analysis neuroscience data, particular focus clinical imaging-derived datasets MRI, PET, derived biomarkers.

title: Statistical analyses procedures from our lab
text: Pre-Post analsysis (delta change analysis, test pre-post, analysis covariance) Correlation analysis (workflow correlation matriz heatmap) Regression models (linear model, logistic model)

title: Statistical analyses procedures from our lab
text: Built-functions statistical checks, exploratory analysis predictive modeling

title: Statistical analyses procedures from our lab
text: Boxplots, violin plots, density plots, histograms continuous categorical variables.

title: correlation_analysis — correlation_analysis
text: correlation_analysis convenient function performs correlation analysis sets variables, calculating correlation coefficients confidence intervals grouped specified variable.

title: correlation_analysis — correlation_analysis
code: correlation_analysis(   input_data = mtcars,   dependent = "mpg",   independent = c("wt", "hp"),   method = "spearman",   group_column = "am" )

title: correlation_analysis — correlation_analysis
text: input_data data.frame containing variables analyze. Defaults mtcars. dependent Vector one several column names use dependent variables. independent Vector one several column names use independent variables. method Correlation method use. Choose "pearson", "spearman", "kendall". Defaults "spearman". group_column Column name identifies different groups data. Defaults "" mtcars example.

title: correlation_analysis — correlation_analysis
text: result data frame correlation results coefficients, confidence intervals, p-values group

title: correlation_analysis — correlation_analysis
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Using the function with default parameters corr_results <- correlation_analysis() # By default uses mtcars data with mpg as dependent and wt, hp as independent print(corr_results) # Table of correlation coefficients, confidence intervals, and p-values } # }

title: Example dataset for analysis — data
text: sample dataset demographic variables smoking status MS patients.

title: Example dataset for analysis — data
text: data frame 146 observations n avriables: age Age years sex Sex ("Male", "Female") smoker Smoking status ("Yes", "")

title: generate_demographic_tables — generate_demographic_tables
text: generate_demographic_tables function creates demographic summary tables calculates statistical tests groups.

title: generate_demographic_tables — generate_demographic_tables
code: generate_demographic_tables(   input_data = mtcars,   parameter_list = c("mpg", "disp", "qsec"),   group_column = "am",   completed_table_statistic = "median",   freeform_brackets = "Q1_Q3",   groupwise_test = "wilcox",   multiple_comparison_correction = "BH",   comparison_list = NULL,   multiple_comparison_correction_brackets = FALSE,   groupwise_95CI = FALSE,   sex_column = "vs",   female_value = 0,   male_value = 1,   completed_table_sex_statistic = "n_m_f",   group_n = TRUE,   format_value = 2,   p_format_value = 3,   mean_if_normal = FALSE,   ttest_if_normal = FALSE,   ordinal_columns = NULL )

title: generate_demographic_tables — generate_demographic_tables
text: input_data data.frame containing variables analyzed. parameter_list Vector column names include analysis. group_column Character string specifying grouping variable. completed_table_statistic statistic show final table. Options: "min_max", "mean", "sd", "median", "min", "max". freeform_brackets (Optional) Add another statistic brackets completed_table_statistic, eg. choose median completed_table_statistic choose Q1-Q3 get "median (Q1-Q3)" Options: "sd", "Q1_Q3", multiple_comparison_correction Method correcting p-values. Options: "holm", "hochberg", "hommel", "bonferroni", "BH", "", "fdr", NULL. comparison_list Optional list specific group comparisons make. sex_column Character string specifying sex variable (optional). female_value Value used identify females sex_column. male_value Value used identify males sex_column. completed_table_sex_statistic sex statistic show final table. Options: "n_m_f" n males n females "n_fn_ratio" n female n n ratio, "n_f_fn_ratio", n female n female n ratio, "f_n_ratio", n female n ratio "n_f", n females "n_m" n males format_value Number decimal places formatting rounded values.

title: generate_demographic_tables — generate_demographic_tables
text: demographic_statistics Raw demographic statistics groups sex_ratios Sex distribution statistics group complete_table Final formatted table suitable publication

title: generate_demographic_tables — generate_demographic_tables
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Using the function results <- generate_demographic_tables() print(results$demographic_statistics) print(results$group_wise_comparisons) print(results$sex_ratios) print(results$complete_table)  } # }

title: run_plsda_clean — run_plsda_clean
text: run_plsda_clean Function cleaning dataset running partial least squares discriminant analysis function separates categorical numeric variables given dataframe scales , runs PLS-DA using mixOmics package. also plots individual samples  variables based cutoff correlation.

title: run_plsda_clean — run_plsda_clean
text: data dataframe containing numeric possibly non-numeric variables. , classify variables according data_for_plsda function group factor vector can coerced factor, representing class labels. ncomp Number PLS components compute. Default 4. var.cutoff Cutoff variable correlation covariates plotVar. Default 0.6.

title: run_plsda_clean — run_plsda_clean
text: list containing plsda model generated plots.

title: run_plsda_clean — run_plsda_clean
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{  library(mixOmics)  # Reading the data data<- MSNeuroLab::data   # Using the function for separating variables  data.plsda <- data_for_plsda(data)   # Using the function for PLSDA plsda <- run_plsda_clean(data = data, group=data$sex, ncomp = 2, var.cutoff = 0.6)   } # }

title: run_univariate_logistic — run_univariate_logistic
text: run_univariate_logistic function performs univariate logistic regression analyses set predictor variables binary outcome variable given dataset. returns tidy data frame odds ratios (), 95% confidence intervals (CI), p-values predictor.

title: run_univariate_logistic — run_univariate_logistic
text: data data frame containing outcome predictor variables. outcome_var string specifying name binary outcome variable. variable factor character, values equal "Yes" recoded 1 others 0. predictors character vector specifying names predictor variables test.

title: run_univariate_logistic — run_univariate_logistic
text: data frame following columns: Predictor Name predictor variable Estimated odds ratio CI_low Lower bound 95% confidence interval CI_high Upper bound 95% confidence interval p_value p-value predictor logistic model

title: run_univariate_logistic — run_univariate_logistic
code: # Reading the data data<- MSNeuroLab::data # Selecting variables to test at univariate  variables_to_test <- colnames(data)[c(3:8)] # Using the function res_univariates <- run_univariate_logistic(data, "smoker", predictors = variables_to_test) #> Waiting for profiling to be done... #> Waiting for profiling to be done... #> Waiting for profiling to be done... #> Waiting for profiling to be done... #> Waiting for profiling to be done... #> Waiting for profiling to be done...

title: run_univariate_logistic_repeated — run_univariate_logistic_repeated
text: run_univariate_logistic_repeated function performs univariate logistic regression analyses set predictor variables binary outcome variable repeated measures given dataset. returns tidy data frame odds ratios (), 95% confidence intervals (CI), p-values predictor.

title: run_univariate_logistic_repeated — run_univariate_logistic_repeated
text: data data frame containing outcome predictor variables. outcome_var string specifying name binary outcome variable. variable factor character, values equal "Yes" recoded 1 others 0. predictors character vector specifying names predictor variables test. time timepoint repeated measures

title: run_univariate_logistic_repeated — run_univariate_logistic_repeated
text: data frame following columns: Predictor Name predictor variable Estimated odds ratio CI_low Lower bound 95% confidence interval CI_high Upper bound 95% confidence interval p_value p-value predictor logistic model

title: run_univariate_logistic_repeated — run_univariate_logistic_repeated
code: # Reading the data data<- MSNeuroLab::data # Selecting variables to test at univariate  variables_to_test <- colnames(data)[c(3:8)] # Using the function res_univariates <- run_univariate_logistic(data, "smoker", predictors = variables_to_test, Time) #> Error in run_univariate_logistic(data, "smoker", predictors = variables_to_test,     Time): unused argument (Time)

title: similarity_analysis — similarity_analysis
text: similarity_analysis function performs similarity matching patients controls based multiple variables. identifies similar control subject patient using distance-based similarity metrics.

title: similarity_analysis — similarity_analysis
code: similarity_analysis(   data = mtcars,   input_columns = c("mpg", "cyl", "hp"),   group_column = "am",   patient_value = 1,   control_value = 0,   comparison_variable = "disp",   distance_metric = "euclidean",   x_label = NULL,   y_label = NULL,   plot_title = NULL,   include_id = FALSE )

title: similarity_analysis — similarity_analysis
text: data data.frame contains numeric categorical variables arranged columns. input_columns List columns used computing distance metrics. group_column Column containing categories two groups whose similarity analysed, example MS patient healthy control. patient_value Value group_column identifies patients. Every patient matched one control. Several patients may match control. control_value Value group_column identifies controls. Every control may match patient. comparison_variable Compare variable patients similar controls. distance_metric Metric used compute difference values. Choose "euclidean", "cosine", "manhattan". x_label Variable plot x-axis. Variable name used NULL. y_label Variable plot y-axis. Variable name used NULL. plot_title Plot title. auto-generated NULL. include_id Whether include patient/control IDs results table.

title: similarity_analysis — similarity_analysis
text: result List table numeric comparison_variable values, distance metrics similarities pairs similar patients controls, scatterplot visualizing comparisons

title: stratified_distance_analysis — stratified_distance_analysis
text: stratified_distance_analysis function performs stratified distance matching patients controls based multiple variables. identifies closest control group patient group using distance-based metrics across defined strata.

title: stratified_distance_analysis — stratified_distance_analysis
code: stratified_distance_analysis(   data = mtcars,   brain_cols = c("mpg", "cyl", "hp"),   group_var = "am",   patient_value = 1,   control_value = 0,   strat_var = "gear",   distance_method = "euclidean",   profile_method = "mean",   plot_title = NULL,   include_counts = TRUE )

title: stratified_distance_analysis — stratified_distance_analysis
text: data data.frame contains numeric categorical variables arranged columns. brain_cols List columns used computing distance metrics. group_var Column containing categories two groups whose distance analysed, example MS patient healthy control. patient_value Value group_var identifies patients. Every patient group matched closest control group. control_value Value group_var identifies controls. strat_var Stratification variable. Column indicating subgroups witch distance assessed matching done. Factorize determine plotting order. distance_method Metric used compute difference values. Choose "euclidean", "cosine", "manhattan". profile_method Method calculate group profiles: difference determined mean median individuals strata. Choose "mean" "median". plot_title Plot title. auto-generated NULL. include_counts Whether include subject counts results table.

title: stratified_distance_analysis — stratified_distance_analysis
text: warnings Non-fatal warnings results Data frame distance metrics patient-control strata combinations top_matches Filtered results showing closest matches patient stratum heatmap Heatmap plot showing distance patient control strata gridplot Grid plot showing closest matches

title: test_with_levene — test_with_levene
text: test_with_levene Function comparing statistical comparison numeric variables, testing assumptions variables' distribution within levels categorical variable

title: test_with_levene — test_with_levene
text: data data.frame object variables (numeric categorical) performing statistical comparisons group_var categorical variable two levels (classes) quotes

title: test_with_levene — test_with_levene
text: final_table data.frame object results unlisted (bindrow function

title: test_with_levene — test_with_levene
code: # Reading the data data<- MSNeuroLab::data # Using the function test_stat <- test_with_levene(data = data[, c(1:4)],  group_var="sex") #> Loading required package: car #> Loading required package: carData #> Loading required package: dplyr #>  #> Attaching package: 'dplyr' #> The following object is masked from 'package:car': #>  #>     recode #> The following objects are masked from 'package:stats': #>  #>     filter, lag #> The following objects are masked from 'package:base': #>  #>     intersect, setdiff, setequal, union #> Loading required package: tibble
